top_failure: Query asked for Tohoku cherry-blossom spots but tool called only for Akita prefecture; geographic scope too narrow for the Tohoku-wide query
improvement: Fan out to all 6 Tohoku prefectures or use search_area with region=Tohoku; apply sakura kind filter
rubric_notes: Spanish query (RULE D): constraint_handling -1 for language mismatch only; scope failure is separate F-category defect; RULE B applied — scope and language penalised in distinct dims

top_failure: Sake-specific query returned mixed results (vegetables, Sado-gold craft, etc.) diluting the sake focus; authoritative MAFF GI nihonshu records are present but buried by off-topic entries
improvement: Filter by category=sake or keyword=日本酒 before returning; rank nihonshu GI entries to the top
rubric_notes: Data is authoritative (MAFF GI) so groundedness/factual stay high; ranking/mixing is a B failure; RULE B: single defect hit on usefulness/intent, not compounded to factual

top_failure: Query explicitly requested ski resorts AND onsen together in Hakuba; response returns only general Nagano spots with zero onsen entries — one of the two core intent requirements entirely missing
improvement: Call get_hotels or get_spots with onsen filter alongside ski-resort spots; merge both layers
rubric_notes: French query (RULE D): constraint_handling -1 for language mismatch only; onsen absence is A-category retrieval failure penalised in intent/usefulness; RULE B: language and retrieval penalised in separate dims

top_failure: Kansai-wide autumn-foliage query served only Kyoto results; Osaka (Minoo), Nara (Yoshino), Hyogo, Shiga foliage absent; no koyo kind filter applied
improvement: Fan out across Kansai prefectures or use search_area with region; apply koyo/momiji kind filter
rubric_notes: English query; Kyoto results are high-quality and authoritative but coverage of the broader Kansai region is the G failure; RULE B: scope and filter issues expressed through intent/constraint, not duplicated in factual

top_failure: Kansai-wide sakura query constrained to Nara prefecture only; Osaka (Mint Bureau), Kyoto (Maruyama), Hyogo (Himeji) absent; scope too narrow for the multi-prefecture request
improvement: Fan out to all Kansai prefectures or use search_area with sakura keyword and Kansai region
rubric_notes: Arabic query (RULE D): constraint_handling -1 for language mismatch; scope constraint is a separate F defect expressed in same dim — combined to 2; Yoshino top result is excellent but does not compensate for geographic gap; RULE B: single dim hit

top_failure: Query specifically asked for dark-sky / stargazing locations on Okinawan remote islands; response returned generic Okinawa heritage spots (castles, shrines, Shuri) with no dark_sky or stargazing kind entries
improvement: Add dark_sky_site kind to data; route intent to filter on outdoor/night-friendly spots; include island-specific entries for Iriomote, Taketomi, Miyako
rubric_notes: Japanese query; retrieval failure — the entire dark-sky entity class is absent from the result set (RULE C: score 2 when entity class entirely absent); groundedness/factual high because returned data is authoritative, just wrong category

top_failure: Query asked for rural / hidden Hokkaido onsen; response mixes 日本秘湯の会 ryokan with urban Sapporo business hotels; rural constraint not applied and language constraint (Thai) not met
improvement: Filter by rural tag or exclude major cities; apply onsen-specific ranking; serve Thai-language fields
rubric_notes: Thai query (RULE D): constraint_handling -1 for language; rural constraint failure is separate F defect — combined in same dim giving 2; 日本秘湯の会 entries are authoritative and partially satisfy intent so intent/usefulness at 3 not 2; RULE B: single dim hit

top_failure: Seto Inland Sea island query restricted to Kagawa; Hiroshima (Ōkunoshima, Tomonoura), Okayama, Hyogo (Awaji) Setouchi islands entirely absent; also language constraint (Vietnamese) not met
improvement: Fan out to all Setouchi prefectures; serve Vietnamese-language fields
rubric_notes: Vietnamese query (RULE D): constraint_handling -1 for language; geographic scope failure is separate F defect — combined to 2; Shōdoshima present and authoritative; RULE B: single dim

top_failure: Query asked for craft-experience workshops for indigo dyeing; response returned the craft designation (阿波正藍しじら織) without venue/workshop booking information — practical action step missing
improvement: Link to Awa Ai workshop venues; include get_spots or get_japan_heritage results for Tokushima craft-experience sites alongside the designation record
rubric_notes: English query; craft data is authoritative METI record; missing actionability for the 'experience' framing is an E-category practicality defect; RULE B: single defect in usefulness only

top_failure: Query in German; response data is English/Japanese only — language constraint unmet
improvement: Serve German-language fields (name_de, description_de) when lang=de detected; Koyasan shukubo data itself is excellent
rubric_notes: German query (RULE D): constraint_handling -1 for language only; all other dims high because data quality is outstanding — Koyasan shukubo from 高野山宿坊協会 is a perfect authoritative match; RULE D strictly applied: language is single -1 in constraint_handling, no cascade

top_failure: Minor: 153 results are comprehensive but not ranked by buke_yashiki prominence; Kakunodate, Chiran, Aizu correctly surfaced
improvement: Boost top-tier buke_yashiki preservation districts to positions 1-5 for quicker agent consumption
rubric_notes: English query; search_area with 武家屋敷 keyword returns broad national coverage across Akita, Kagoshima, Fukushima, Shimane — all dims ≥4 so failure_category null; RULE E: no featured_cluster present

top_failure: Akita summer festival query returned only 3 Akita festival entries; Kanto Matsuri (the iconic lantern-balloon festival, August, Akita City) absent from results; national_heritage section mixes non-Akita prefectures
improvement: Ensure Kanto Matsuri is indexed in Akita festivals data; filter national_heritage section to match prefecture; sort by month for summer relevance
rubric_notes: Indonesian query served; coverage gap (Kanto Matsuri absent) is the primary G failure; language constraint partially met (en fields returned); RULE B: coverage failure in intent/usefulness, not duplicated

top_failure: Query asked for Sanin fishing port towns; response returned Shimane castle/shrine/nature spots with no fishing village or port town entries — entity class entirely absent
improvement: Add fishing_village and harbor_town kinds to data; route fishing-port intent to coastal/port clusters
rubric_notes: Korean query (RULE D): constraint_handling -1 for language; fishing-port entity class entirely absent is A-category retrieval failure (RULE C: score 2 for absent class); RULE B: language and retrieval failures in separate dims

top_failure: Query asked for Gifu washi experience (hands-on papermaking); response returned craft designation (美濃和紙) without venue or workshop booking information for the experience component
improvement: Supplement get_local_specialty results with get_spots or get_japan_heritage for Mino washi craft-experience venues (Udatsu no Machinami area has papermaking workshops)
rubric_notes: English query; 美濃和紙 is an excellent and authoritative METI match; the practicality gap for 'experience' framing is an E defect; usefulness drops because agent cannot action the workshop step; RULE B: single defect

top_failure: Noto fishing village query returned Kanazawa-heavy general Ishikawa spots; no Noto Peninsula fishing villages (Wajima, Suzu, Monzen) present — entity class and sub-region both absent
improvement: Resolve Noto Peninsula as a sub-region and filter spots to the Noto area; add fishing_village kind for coastal Noto spots
rubric_notes: Spanish query (RULE D): constraint_handling -1 for language; Noto fishing village entity class entirely absent is A-category failure; RULE B: language and retrieval in separate dims; combined constraint score 2

top_failure: Query asked for sand dunes 'not just Tottori'; Tottori Sand Dunes appear first and dominate; Fukiage, Hamaoka, Uchinadakaigan present but buried — ranking does not reflect the explicit non-Tottori preference
improvement: Detect negative geographic constraint in query; de-boost Tottori when user specifies 'not just Tottori'; promote other sand dune locations to positions 1-3
rubric_notes: English query; coverage of alternative dunes is good (B not G) but ordering fails the explicit constraint — factual_accuracy docked 1 for mis-ranking relative to stated preference; RULE B: ranking failure in specificity/intent, not compounded to factual

top_failure: San'in craft query (Shimane + Tottori) returned only 4 Shimane crafts; Tottori crafts (因州和紙, 弓浜絣) absent — coverage incomplete for the two-prefecture San'in scope
improvement: Fan out to Tottori as well as Shimane when San'in region is requested; ensure Inshuwashi and Yumihama-gasuri are indexed in Tottori craft data
rubric_notes: Chinese query served; Shimane data is authoritative and well-structured; Tottori absence is a G-category coverage gap; usefulness stays 4 because returned Shimane data is actionable; RULE B: coverage failure in intent, not duplicated across dims

top_failure: Hokkaido lavender query returned generic Hokkaido heritage spots (castles, shrines, parks) with zero lavender or flower-farm entries — the core intent (lavender) is entirely absent from the result set
improvement: Add lavender_farm and flower_farm kinds to data; index Furano and Biei lavender fields; route lavender intent to seasonal/botanical cluster
rubric_notes: English query; lavender entity class entirely absent (RULE C: score 2 → score 1 for complete retrieval failure when the intent is single-topic); groundedness/factual high because returned authoritative data, just categorically wrong; intent/usefulness at 1 reflecting complete miss

top_failure: Tagalog query about Kyushu old townscapes (古い町並み); tool called get_spots for Oita returning generic castle/shrine/waterfall results with no preserved streetscape or historic townscape entries; Tagalog language constraint also unmet
improvement: Resolve 古い町並み intent to preservation-district kind; fan out across Kyushu; serve Tagalog-language fields
rubric_notes: Tagalog query (RULE D): constraint_handling -1 for language; historic townscape entity class entirely absent in Oita results (even Usuki historic district absent) — A-category retrieval failure; intent 1 because both the entity class and prefecture scope are wrong; RULE B: language and retrieval in separate dims

top_failure: Hokuriku kominka hotel query returned Ishikawa-only results; Toyama and Fukui kominka accommodations absent; within results, Bed and Craft (kominka hotel) present but buried under conventional hotels
improvement: Fan out to all three Hokuriku prefectures; apply kominka/machiya filter; rank traditional-guesthouse entries first
rubric_notes: Japanese query; Bed and Craft is an authoritative kominka match but ranking and scope are B-category failures; coverage gap (Toyama, Fukui) is secondary; RULE B: ranking and scope expressed through intent/specificity, not duplicated

top_failure: Kii Peninsula mountain-faith heritage query returned excellent Kumano Sanzan / Kodo / Kongōbu-ji data but the large browse_advisory navigation block reduces agent-usable density; practical pilgrimage logistics (distance, overnight options) absent
improvement: Reduce browse_advisory verbosity or move to a secondary field; add feasibility notes (pilgrimage duration, access from Osaka/Nagoya) to key heritage items
rubric_notes: English query; get_japan_heritage is the correct tool and data is authoritative; E-category practicality gap because the agent gets the heritage narrative but not pilgrimage logistics; expression_quality 3 for the verbose nav-chrome overhead

top_failure: Sea of Japan coast fireworks query for full coastline returned only Niigata results; Nagaoka Fireworks (one of Japan's top 3, on the Japan Sea side) present but buried in 129 items; Toyama, Akita, Yamagata fireworks absent
improvement: Fan out to all Sea of Japan prefectures when a coastline-wide query is detected; rank Nagaoka and Ojiya fireworks to top positions for Niigata results
rubric_notes: English query; Nagaoka Fireworks is present (B not A/G) but ranking and scope both weak; RULE B: ranking and scope failures expressed in intent/specificity, not duplicated across other dims

top_failure: Minor: 博多織 (Fukuoka) included alongside Oita-specific crafts; slightly dilutes prefecture focus
improvement: When prefecture filter is set, rank prefecture-matched records strictly above cross-prefecture entries
rubric_notes: English query; 別府竹細工 is a perfect METI match for Oita bamboo craft; all dims ≥4 so failure_category null; no featured_cluster so RULE E does not apply

top_failure: Shikoku cycling query returned Ehime-only spots; Shimanami Kaidō (present) is the standout cycling anchor but Kochi Shimanto river cycling, Tokushima, Kagawa cycling routes entirely absent; most returned spots are castles/shrines with no cycling relevance
improvement: Fan out to all 4 Shikoku prefectures; apply cycling_route kind filter to surface cycling-specific spots; rank Shimanami Kaidō to position 1
rubric_notes: English query; Shimanami Kaidō and Ōmishima (cycling_route kind) are present and authoritative but buried; scope restriction to Ehime for a 'Shikoku' query is a G-coverage failure; RULE B: scope and filter issues expressed in intent/specificity

top_failure: None significant; browse_advisory nav-chrome adds minor verbosity overhead
improvement: Minor: trim cross_program_advisory to kabuki-relevant subcategory only when kabuki keyword is detected, to reduce token consumption
rubric_notes: English query; RULE E applied: featured_cluster (performing_arts / kabuki subcategory in cross_program_advisory) is present and directly answers the query → +1 to intent_understanding (4→5) and specificity (4→5); 金丸座/内子座/八千代座/康楽館 are all outside Tokyo+Kyoto; constraint fully met; all dims ≥4 so failure_category null
